page: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled) and a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

The defining characteristic of the d3.2xlarge is its local storage capacity, offering 11880 GB of HDD storage. This massive storage volume is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance and average memory bandwidth, with read-only large block memory bandwidth measured at 51.95 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and database operations perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 6.8 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d3.2xlarge provides high storage density per resource unit, making it a viable option for workloads requiring massive local capacity without the premium cost of SSDs. The primary tradeoff is the lower sequential and random I/O performance of HDD storage compared to NVMe SSDs, alongside the weaker single-threaded compute capabilities. This profile is best suited for large-scale data warehousing, map-reduce jobs, log processing, and massive file systems.
description: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a substantial 11880 GB of local HDD storage. Operating at 3.1 GHz, the CPU delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core benchmark results. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps and lacks GPU acceleration. Qualitatively, the d3.2xlarge offers high storage density, trading SSD-level speed for massive HDD capacity. This hardware profile is optimized for storage-intensive workloads, such as distributed file systems, log processing, and data warehousing, where capacity is prioritized over high-speed random I/O.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 11880 GB of local HDD storage, designed for high-capacity data warehousing and log processing workloads.

tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for data-intensive processing workloads.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
